name: Engineers India Limited
oneLine: Indexed directory listing exposes CPP-Angul-Smelter project files; Apache 2.4.29 EOL; no SPF/DMARC
findings: Public directory listing on /wp-content/uploads/cache/2019/01/CPP-ANGUL-SMELTER_full/ — Google-indexed, project documents exposed
No SPF, no DMARC — both entirely absent

urgentActions.what: Submit Google de-index request for the exposed CPP-Angul-Smelter folder

eolFlags: SPF and DMARC entirely absent — open spoofing surface
Apache 2.4.29 EOL on legacy edge (if still in stack)

attackPaths.name: Email spoofing via missing SPF/DMARC
attackPaths.entry: No SPF, no DMARC. Spoofed @engineersindia.com with no policy enforcement.
attackPaths.pivot: Executive clicks phishing link → credentials harvested.
attackPaths.objective: Initial compromise; lateral move via trust.

attackPaths.name: Typosquat + spoofed domain
attackPaths.pivot: Send spoofed mail; SPF absence = pass; high-trust exploitation.

attackPaths.name: Cached document lure
attackPaths.entry: Google Cache may still show CPP-Angul-Smelter PDF filenames despite directory now redirecting.
attackPaths.pivot: Craft identical-looking memo (Design Review Q1 2026); email technical staff; RAT deployment on click.

attackPaths.name: Apache 2.4.29 unpatched CVEs (if still serving)
attackPaths.entry: If Apache 2.4.29 remains on the stack, CVE-2024-47252 / CVE-2024-43204 / CVE-2024-42516 apply.
attackPaths.pivot: TLS 1.3 access bypass; mod_rewrite SSRF; mod_proxy SSRF.

paths.preAi: Missing SPF/DMARC + cached governance doc filenames → credential compromise + supply-chain intel exfil
paths.mythos: AI-augmented attacker auto-correlates cached filenames with current project cycles and crafts indistinguishable lures

paths.preAi: Manual lure crafting from cached document index
paths.mythos: Cached document index becomes a phishing-template generator

cisoPatchList.title: Implement SPF record (hard -all)
cisoPatchList.fix: Publish: v=spf1 include:_spf.<actual mail provider> -all. Audit mail provider; align before enforcement.

cisoPatchList.title: Implement DMARC p=quarantine → p=reject after 30 days
cisoPatchList.fix: Publish DMARC with rua=. Monitor 30 days; promote to p=reject.

cisoPatchList.title: Submit Google Search Console de-index request for cached cache/* paths
cisoPatchList.fix: GSC → Removals → submit /wp-content/uploads/cache/*. Add robots.txt block to prevent re-index.

cisoPatchList.title: Audit and upgrade WordPress / plugins / Apache
cisoPatchList.fix: wp-cli: wp plugin list --update=available. Apply updates. Apache to 2.4.62+ if 2.4.29 still present. Test in staging.
cisoPatchList.validation: Apache 2.4.62+ in headers; wp plugin status all-current

cisoPatchList.title: Deploy WAF — block directory traversal, SQLi, WP exploit patterns
Implement DKIM signing for outbound mail
cisoPatchList.fix: Mail server config: enable DKIM signing. Publish public key in DNS.
cisoPatchList.validation: Test email shows DKIM-Signature: pass in headers

phase2.headlineQuestion: Can EIL implement SPF/DMARC and CSP headers, and submit a Google de-index request, before the next spear-phishing campaign uses cached project filenames as lures?
